---
name: Update CI Dependencies
description: >-
  Refreshes pinned CI/CD dependencies – SHA-pinned GitHub Actions in
  .github/workflows/, task versions and 1ES template refs in
  .github/azure-devops/ – and audits Node.js runtime consistency across
  workflows, pipelines, package.json, and .nvmrc. Triggered when
  Release – Initiate completes so updates land on the release pull request
  it opens; workflow_dispatch is available as a manual retrigger while a
  release pull request is open.

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows:
      - "Release – Initiate"
    types:
      - completed
    branches:
      - "**"
  workflow_dispatch: null

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read
  actions: read

engine: copilot

tools:
  edit:
  bash:
    - "git *"
    - "gh api *"
    - "gh pr list *"
    - "gh pr view *"
    - "jq *"

checkout:
  fetch:
    - "*"
  fetch-depth: 0

safe-outputs:
  push-to-pull-request-branch:
    target: "*"
    labels:
      - release
    max: 1
    if-no-changes: ignore
    protected-files:
      policy: fallback-to-issue
      exclude:
        - package.json
        - .nvmrc
    github-token-for-extra-empty-commit: ${{ secrets.PR_METRICS_TOKEN }}
---

# Update CI Dependencies

Implements the `update-ci-dependencies` skill. Read
`.github/skills/update-ci-dependencies/SKILL.md` and follow its **Process**,
**Node.js Version Consistency**, **Output Format**, and **Constraints**
sections exactly. This workflow file adds only the invocation-specific
rules below.

## Invocation Context

- Triggered by `Release – Initiate` completing or by a manual
  `workflow_dispatch`.
- Target: the open pull request that carries the `release` label (created
  by `Release – Initiate`, with title `[Autogenerated] Release v<version>`).
- If no pull request with the `release` label is currently open, stop and
  produce no output. Do not open a new pull request.

## Invocation-Specific Overrides

- **Commit Message**: Use `chore: refresh CI pins`, not the
  dependency-class-specific message the skill suggests, because a single
  invocation may span multiple classes.
- **Output Target**: Push onto the existing release pull request's branch.
  The `push-to-pull-request-branch` safe output in the frontmatter handles
  this – do not call `gh pr create` yourself.
- **Allowed Writes**: Only `.github/workflows/*.yml`,
  `.github/azure-devops/*.yml`, `package.json`, and `.nvmrc`. Everything
  outside this set is off-limits for this invocation.
- **Node.js Version**: Audit only. If values diverge, align them on the
  pin from the most recently bumped file (per `git log`). Never change the
  value itself from this workflow.

## Verification

No manual verification is required. After the push, the `Build` workflow
reruns automatically (via the empty commit made with `PR_METRICS_TOKEN`)
and re-executes Super-Linter against the modified files. Any lint or
typing failure surfaces there.
